_meta.description: Structured reference for the beaver Needs and Well-Being system. Values compiled from the official Timberborn wiki (timberborn.wiki.gg). Numbers marked with _uncertain were not confirmed with an exact in-game figure.

conventions.satisfaction_scale: Most needs run on a 0-100 satisfaction meter (Shelter runs -20..80, Sleep 0..80). Decay is subtracted from the meter each in-game day; visiting the source refills it.
conventions.wellbeing_bonus_value: The maximum well-being SCORE points a fully-satisfied need contributes to the beaver's total Well-Being score. A beaver only earns the points while the underlying meter is above 0.

source_buildings: Any Food source: Gathering Flag / bushes (berries), Farmhouse crops, Grill/Bakery/Gristmill processed foods, food distributed and eaten anywhere in a district
survival_needs.what_happens_if_unmet: At 0 Hunger the beaver gains the 'Starving' status: Adults -50% Work Speed, Kits -40% Growth Speed. Continued starvation kills the beaver.
survival_needs.time_to_death: ~5 days from a full meter (about 1.25 days to empty at 80/day, then ~3.75 days of Starving before death).
survival_needs.notes: Food is the single most important logistic. Eating different food TYPES also feeds the Nutrition well-being need (see wellbeing_needs).

source_buildings: Water Pump / Deep Water Pump (draws water) -> Water is stored and distributed; beavers drink from stock anywhere in the district
Large Water Tank / Water Tank for storage
survival_needs.what_happens_if_unmet: At 0 Thirst the beaver becomes 'Dehydrated': -25% Movement Speed. Continued dehydration kills the beaver.
survival_needs.time_to_death: ~5.71 days from a full meter (about 1.43 days to empty at 70/day, then ~4.3 days dehydrated).
survival_needs.notes: A Water Pump must sit on/next to a water tile. Water is also needed for many production chains and irrigation.

source_buildings: Housing (Lodge, Mini Lodge, Double Lodge, Triple Lodge) - sheltered sleep
District Center provides a fallback sleep spot but with the homeless/shelter penalty
survival_needs.restore_rate: +30 per hour while sleeping in housing; one night of sheltered sleep refills the meter from min to max.
survival_needs.what_happens_if_unmet: -10% Movement Speed and reduced sleep restoration; if fully depleted the beaver falls asleep on the spot wherever it is.
survival_needs.notes: Beavers only sleep during the night portion of their schedule. Housing satisfies Sleep AND the Shelter need together.

survival_needs.category_note: Listed as a basic need; also feeds Well-Being (see wellbeing_needs entry 'Shelter/Housing'). Included here because lack of shelter degrades survival-critical sleep and blocks breeding.
survival_needs.restore_rate: +30 per hour while sleeping in housing.
survival_needs.what_happens_if_unmet: Homeless beavers sleep poorly: ~-25% sleep restoration (need longer to recover) and a negative well-being contribution. Folktails CANNOT reproduce without adequate shelter/free housing.
survival_needs.notes: Because it gates breeding and good sleep, treat housing as survival-tier at game start.

source_buildings: Eating different TYPES of food. Each distinct food eaten contributes well-being; e.g. Berries, Carrots, Potatoes, Grilled/Baked/processed goods, Maple Syrup products, Coffee.
wellbeing_needs.wellbeing_bonus_value: +1 to +2 per common food type; premium items (Maple Pastry, Coffee) +3. Total scales with the variety of foods available in the district.
wellbeing_needs.notes: Early game a single food source gives little/no Nutrition well-being; adding a second and third food type is one of the cheapest ways to raise the score. Except Coffee, all Nutrition foods also satisfy Hunger.

source_buildings: Water tiles (walking through flowing water via paths) - free but risks Contamination on Badtides
wellbeing_needs.notes: One of the first well-being needs to address; cheap. Shower is the safe, reliable source vs. raw water tiles.

wellbeing_needs.radius: Beavers walk to and gather at the building during leisure time; multiple visitors present improves the effect.
wellbeing_needs.notes: Campfire is the cheapest early Social Life source. Only the single highest-tier Social Life building a beaver visits counts toward the score (needs of the same category do not stack across buildings).

wellbeing_needs.radius: Proximity-based: the beaver must pass near/within range of the decoration. Exact tile radius not documented.
wellbeing_needs.notes: Passive - beavers top up Aesthetics just by walking past decorations placed along paths. Only the strongest in-range decoration of the category counts (no stacking).

wellbeing_needs.radius: Proximity to the monument. Exact radius not documented.
wellbeing_needs.notes: High-value but expensive/late-game. Wonder-tier (Earth Recultivator) barely decays (-1/day) so a single visit sustains +10 for a long time.

wellbeing_needs.radius: Beavers walk to and use the building during leisure time.
wellbeing_needs.notes: Lido double-dips: it satisfies Wet Fur AND Fun. Only the highest-tier Fun building visited counts.

wellbeing_needs.radius: n/a (beaver sleeps in an assigned dwelling)
wellbeing_needs.notes: A sheltered beaver gets up to +1 well-being; a homeless beaver gets up to -3 (net penalty). Also a hard gate on Folktails breeding. See survival_needs 'Shelter' for sleep effects.

source_buildings: Library-type building where beavers read Books
wellbeing_needs.radius: Beaver visits the reading building during leisure.
wellbeing_needs.notes: Folktails-specific. Reading Books contributes to leisure/Fun-type well-being. Provides a distinct advancement path vs Iron Teeth. Exact point values not confirmed for v1.0.x.

wellbeing_summary.definition: Well-Being is a per-beaver score = sum of the well-being bonus points from every satisfied need. Higher score unlocks stat bonuses. Reaching Well-Being 15 (Folktails) is required to unlock the Iron Teeth faction.
wellbeing_summary.stacking_rule: Within a single need category, only the highest-value satisfied source counts (a Campfire + Dance Hall do not add together). Across DIFFERENT categories (Social Life + Aesthetics + Fun + ...), the points add up.
bonus_notes: Work Speed bonus applies to Adults; Growth Speed bonus applies to Kits (kits mature faster at higher well-being).
Movement Speed also improves with well-being though the wiki table is sparse on exact per-tier movement values.
Life Expectancy is the biggest lever: at very high well-being (70+) an adult can gain up to roughly +260 extra days of life on top of the ~50-day base.
Exact per-row mapping was partly reconstructed from the wiki table and should be treated as approximate.

min_wellbeing_to_grow_population.explanation: Folktails breed as long as survival needs are met, there is free housing, and well-being is positive (>0). There is no hard high threshold, but higher well-being directly increases breeding SPEED. Negative conditions (starving/dehydrated) or no free housing halt breeding entirely.

population_growth.mechanism: Natural, housing-based breeding. Two Adult beavers sharing a dwelling can produce a Kit over time when conditions are favorable. New beavers are born as Kits.
requirements: A dwelling (Lodge etc.) occupied by at least two Adults.
Free housing capacity for the new Kit (colony will not reproduce with no room).
Survival needs met - starvation or dehydration halts breeding.
Positive well-being - the higher the well-being, the faster kits appear.

rate.description: Probabilistic per occupied dwelling over time; scales up with well-being score and number of adult pairs / occupied dwellings. No fixed period documented.

population_growth.iron_teeth_contrast: Iron Teeth do NOT breed naturally - they use industrial Breeding Pods, each sustaining ~10 beavers (scaling with life expectancy). Included only for contrast; the bot targets Folktails.
population_growth.notes: For Folktails, building more housing + raising well-being (Campfire, Mud Pit, food variety) directly accelerates growth.
